top_signals.title: Triton Is Said to Near €3 Billion Deal for Carlyle’s Flender
top_signals.summary: Triton Partners is nearing a deal to buy Flender GmbH, a provider of mechanical and electrical drive technology, in a transaction valuing the firm at about €3 billion ($3.5 billion), according to people familiar with the matter.
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.

top_signals.title: How do you teach a robotaxi London? Waymo explains
top_signals.summary: Before Waymo launches a commercial robotaxi service in London, it needs to teach its vehicles more than just how to drive on the left, writes Saskia Koopman. Waymo, the Alphabet-owned autonomous driving company, has spent months testing vehicles across the capital ahead of a planned commercial launch, collecting driving data on the capital’s streets and [...]

top_signals.title: Losses widen at UK fintech Monese in eight month delayed accounts
top_signals.summary: UK fintech Monese suffered a major widening of losses as its bottom line was hit by restructuring costs amidst its takeover by pseudo-bank peer Pockit. The London-based firm – which provides mobile-only current accounts, multi-currency wallets, and money transfer services – booked a pre-tax loss of £15.9m in the 2024 financial year. Fresh accounts – [...]
